title: Lavar Johnson

infobox.value: (born June 11, 1977) is an American bare-knuckle boxer and former [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight_(MMA) "Heavyweight (MMA)") [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional from 2004 until 2014, he competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), the [WEC](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") and [Bellator MMA](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**Lavar Johnson** (born June 11, 1977) is an American bare-knuckle boxer and former [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight_(MMA) "Heavyweight (MMA)") [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional from 2004 until 2014, he competed for the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), the [WEC](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") and [Bellator MMA](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Johnson is from [Madera, California](/wiki/Madera,_California "Madera, California") and was a star football player, attending [Madera High School](/wiki/Madera_High_School "Madera High School") where a coach nicknamed Johnson "Black Superman."[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Johnson then continued his career excelling as an outside [linebacker](/wiki/Linebacker "Linebacker") and strong safety at [Merced College](/wiki/Merced_College "Merced College") before playing at the semi-professional level. Johnson competed in [Toughman Contests](/wiki/Toughman_Contest "Toughman Contest") and dominated, leading him to transition into professional mixed martial arts.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### World Extreme Cagefighting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: World Extreme Cagefighting")]

Johnson made his professional mixed martial arts debut in the WEC on January 16, 2004, losing to future [WEC Light Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_WEC_champions#Light_Heavyweight_Championship "List of WEC champions") [Doug Marshall](/wiki/Doug_Marshall "Doug Marshall") via [TKO](/wiki/Technical_knockout "Technical knockout") (corner stoppage). The bout was somewhat marred by controversy as Johnson was on the receiving end of four low blows over the course of the bout, which was eventually stopped due to exhaustion on his part. Johnson had entered the bout without any training or preparation beyond hitting a heavy bag.[[5]](#cite_note-5) He next defeated Levi Thornbrue via TKO (punches). He knocked out Fred Diaz with punches at [WEC 11: Evolution](/wiki/WEC_11 "WEC 11"). Johnson then fought twice outside of WEC, winning both bouts via first round stoppage. He returned to face Corey Salter at [WEC 17: Halloween Fury 4](/wiki/WEC_17 "WEC 17"), making him submit to strikes in the first round. In Johnson's final WEC fight, he lost to the final [WEC Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_WEC_champions#Heavyweight_Championship "List of WEC champions") [Brian Olsen](/wiki/Brian_Olsen "Brian Olsen") via submission due to a knee injury.

After the loss to Olsen, and because of the knee injury sustained during that loss, Johnson briefly left mixed martial arts to become a delivery driver for [Budweiser](/wiki/Budweiser_(Anheuser-Busch) "Budweiser (Anheuser-Busch)"). Johnson claimed that the cost of his medical expenses, coupled with his inability to train and fight, because of his injury, necessitated that he go get a "real" job. He returned to fighting after a two-and a-half year layoff, with a submission loss to Alijah Reni.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

He went on to defeat his next four opponents via first round stoppage, before being signed by [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)").

### Strikeforce

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Strikeforce")]

Johnson made his [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") debut against [Carl Seumanutafa](/wiki/Carl_Seumanutafa "Carl Seumanutafa") at [Strikeforce Challengers: Evangelista vs. Aina](/wiki/Strikeforce_Challengers:_Evangelista_vs._Aina "Strikeforce Challengers: Evangelista vs. Aina"), knocking him out with a punch 18 seconds into the first round. Johnson knocked out Seumanutafa with an [uppercut](/wiki/Uppercut "Uppercut") while Seumanutafa was going for a takedown.

Johnson was shot on July 4, 2009, while at a family reunion. Johnson was rumored to return to action at [Strikeforce Challengers: Kaufman vs. Hashi](/wiki/Strikeforce_Challengers:_Kaufman_vs._Hashi "Strikeforce Challengers: Kaufman vs. Hashi"), facing [Lolohea Mahe](/wiki/Lolohea_Mahe "Lolohea Mahe"). The match was instead moved to [Strikeforce Challengers: Johnson vs. Mahe](/wiki/Strikeforce_Challengers:_Johnson_vs._Mahe "Strikeforce Challengers: Johnson vs. Mahe"), as the main event. Johnson won by TKO due to punches in the second round.

He next faced current [Bellator](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA") fighter [Virgil Zwicker](/wiki/Virgil_Zwicker "Virgil Zwicker") at [Strikeforce Challengers: Bowling vs. Voelker](/wiki/List_of_Strikeforce_events "List of Strikeforce events"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) knocking him out with punches in the first round.

Johnson fought in a [Strikeforce Heavyweight Grand Prix Tournament](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts)#Strikeforce_Grand_Prix "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)") alternate match, submitting to [Shane Del Rosario](/wiki/Shane_Del_Rosario "Shane Del Rosario")'s [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar") in the first round.

Johnson's next fight came against former [Louisiana State University](/wiki/Louisiana_State_University "Louisiana State University") football player, [Shawn Jordan](/wiki/Shawn_Jordan "Shawn Jordan") at [Strikeforce Challengers: Larkin vs. Rossborough](/wiki/List_of_Strikeforce_events "List of Strikeforce events"). Johnson lost via submission to an [Americana](/wiki/Armlock#Top_shoulder_lock "Armlock") in the second round. Afterwards, he took time off to heal injuries to his right elbow and left knee.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Johnson made his UFC debut on January 28, 2012, defeating [Joey Beltran](/wiki/Joey_Beltran "Joey Beltran") via first-round KO at [UFC on Fox: Evans vs. Davis](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Evans_vs._Davis "UFC on Fox: Evans vs. Davis").[[9]](#cite_note-9) This earned him the *Knockout of the Night* bonus award.

Johnson returned just over three months later for his second UFC fight, defeating fan-favorite [Pat Barry](/wiki/Pat_Barry_(fighter) "Pat Barry (fighter)") via first-round TKO at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[10]](#cite_note-10) For this, he was awarded his second consecutive *Knockout of the Night* bonus.

Johnson replaced an injured [Mark Hunt](/wiki/Mark_Hunt "Mark Hunt") on ten days' notice[[11]](#cite_note-11) and was defeated by [Stefan Struve](/wiki/Stefan_Struve "Stefan Struve") on the main card of [UFC 146](/wiki/UFC_146 "UFC 146") via submission due to an [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar") in the first round.

Johnson was expected to face [Brendan Schaub](/wiki/Brendan_Schaub "Brendan Schaub") on December 8, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_5 "UFC on Fox 5").[[12]](#cite_note-12) However, Johnson was forced out of the bout with a groin injury and Schaub was pulled from the card altogether.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Johnson/Schaub eventually took place on February 23, 2013, at [UFC 157](/wiki/UFC_157 "UFC 157").[[14]](#cite_note-14) Johnson lost the fight via unanimous decision. Johnson was subsequently released from the organization after a post fight drug screening revealed elevated levels of testosterone. His fighting license was suspended by the [California State Athletic Commission](/wiki/California_State_Athletic_Commission "California State Athletic Commission") till November 23, 2013. Johnson admitted he was undergoing [testosterone replacement therapy](/wiki/Testosterone_replacement_therapy "Testosterone replacement therapy") and had not disclosed this on his pre-fight medical questionnaire.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Bellator MMA

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Bellator MMA")]

Following his release from the UFC, Johnson signed with Bellator MMA. He debuted on October 4, 2013, in the heavyweight tournament against [Vinicius Queiroz](/wiki/Vinicius_Queiroz "Vinicius Queiroz") at [Bellator 102](/wiki/Bellator_MMA:_Season_Nine#Bellator_CII "Bellator MMA: Season Nine"). In a surprising upset, Johnson was stopped by one punch just 23 seconds into the fight, marking the first time he had been knocked out in his career.

Johnson returned to the Bellator cage in March 2014 as a participant in the [Bellator Season Ten Heavyweight Tournament](/wiki/Bellator_MMA:_Season_Ten#Heavyweight_tournament_bracket "Bellator MMA: Season Ten"). He faced [Ryan Martinez](/wiki/Ryan_Martinez_(fighter) "Ryan Martinez (fighter)") in the opening round at [Bellator 111](/wiki/Bellator_111 "Bellator 111") on March 7, 2014. He won the fight via knockout in the first round. Johnson faced [Blagoy Ivanov](/wiki/Blagoy_Ivanov "Blagoy Ivanov") in the semifinals at [Bellator 116](/wiki/Bellator_116 "Bellator 116") on April 11, 2014. He lost the back-and-forth bout via submission in the first round.

Johnson faced [Cheick Kongo](/wiki/Cheick_Kongo "Cheick Kongo") on September 5, 2014, at [Bellator 123](/wiki/Bellator_MMA_in_2014#Bellator_123 "Bellator MMA in 2014").[[16]](#cite_note-16) He lost the fight via rear-naked choke submission in the first round.

## Bare-knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing")]

### Valor Bare Knuckle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Valor Bare Knuckle")]

On September 21, 2019, Johnson returned to professional fighting via bare-knuckle boxing, defeating former [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") and Ultimate Fighter veteran [James McSweeney](/wiki/James_McSweeney "James McSweeney") via first-round TKO at Valor Bare Knuckle 1.

### Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship")]

Johnson was scheduled to face [Rashad Coulter](/wiki/Rashad_Coulter "Rashad Coulter") followed by Bobby Brents on November 23, 2024, at [BKFC Fight Night 18](/wiki/BKFC_Fight_Night_18 "BKFC Fight Night 18").[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, both Coulter and Brents withdrew for unknown reasons and was replaced by Anthony Garrett. Johnson lost by knockout 12 seconds into the first round.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Johnson has two sons.

### Legal issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Legal issues")]

In 2007, Johnson was sentenced to 14 days of jail and three years of probation as well as ordered to undergo alcohol counselling after pleading not guilty to a misdemeanor charges of corporal injury on a spouse/co-habitant. He was then jailed for 30 days for criminal threats and disobeying a domestic relations court order.

On July 5, 2008, at approximately midnight, Johnson was shot (along with four others, one fatally) at a family reunion in [Bakersfield, California](/wiki/Bakersfield,_California "Bakersfield, California").[[19]](#cite_note-19) According to reports released by the [Bakersfield Police Department](/wiki/Bakersfield_Police_Department "Bakersfield Police Department"), a male in his late teens approached Johnson and the other victims as they were gathering in the front yard during a family reunion/[Independence Day](/wiki/Independence_Day_(United_States) "Independence Day (United States)") celebration and opened fire on the crowd at random, shooting five people. Johnson was shot in the hip, the forearm, and the third bullet fired at him hit his abdomen, which was the most serious injury. Johnson's [American Kickboxing Academy](/wiki/American_Kickboxing_Academy "American Kickboxing Academy") trainer, Bob Cook, told Sherdog.com shortly afterwards that Johnson was between serious and critical condition. Cook stated he believed Johnson had been struck twice, once in the abdomen and once in the chest. His [appendix](/wiki/Vermiform_appendix "Vermiform appendix") was removed and he could not eat or drink for a month after the surgery which sewed his stomach closed. In August 2008, Johnson was released from hospital, and was said to be doing well and hoping to return to training as soon as possible. A fifteen-year-old girl was later arrested in connection to the shooting. Johnson has a scar up and down his torso as a result of the surgery and his cousin, Anthony, who was from [Jacksonville, Texas](/wiki/Jacksonville,_Texas "Jacksonville, Texas") was the casualty.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

On August 25, 2015, Johnson was arrested in [Fresno, California](/wiki/Fresno,_California "Fresno, California") and charged with corporal injury on a spouse/cohabitant, assault by means likely to produce great bodily injury, dissuading a witness from reporting a crime and dissuading a witness by force. He had allegedly repeatedly punched, kneed and slammed his girlfriend on August 11, before fleeing the scene. He was in Fresno County Jail with bail set at $175,000. He pled not guilty to all charges, which carry a maximum sentence of 10 years and eight months in prison.[[22]](#cite_note-22) On April 29, 2016, Johnson was sentenced to five years in prison for the August 2015 assault.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lavar_Johnson&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **War Gods MMA**
  + War Gods Heavyweight Championship (One time)
* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + [Knockout Of The Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (Two times)
